sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Fulham have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, securing 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, with a notable ability to score late as seen in recent games. Brighton & Hove Albion are in decent form, remaining unbeaten in their last 5 matches with 2 wins and 3 draws, displaying resilience with late goals and consistent scoring.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, matches between Fulham and Brighton have been tight, with one draw (1-1) and a narrow Brighton victory (2-1). Both teams have scored in these games, indicating a trend of competitive, goal-scoring fixtures.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: Fulham's Harry Wilson has been in excellent form, averaging a 7.74 Sofascore rating over the last 5 matches with 3 goals and 1 assist. Brighton's Jan Paul van Hecke stands out with a strong average rating of 7.38 this season and has contributed 3 goals and 2 assists, making him a key defensive and offensive threat.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Fulham, sitting 11th with 31 points, have struggled with big chances created (18th in the league) but excel in ground duels won (3rd). Brighton, in 12th with 30 points, have a slightly better attacking output with 32 goals scored and often score late in matches, as shown in their goal distribution.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Fulham might exploit Brighton's lack of clean sheets (3 in last 5 matches) by pushing for goals, especially with their late-scoring tendency. Brighton, with a strong possession game (often over 60%), could dominate the midfield but must be wary of Fulham's tackling prowess (7th in tackles per game).

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given the close Sofascore probabilities (Fulham 39%, Draw 24%, Brighton 37%) and recent form, this match is likely to be tight. Considering both teams' scoring trends and defensive vulnerabilities, a draw seems plausible, with both sides likely to find the net.
